course_info.title: Business Internship
course_info.meeting_schedule: Biweekly seminar - 1 hour + minimum 120 hours at internship site

learning_objectives: Understand internship requirements, expectations, and grading criteria
Develop a learning agreement with specific professional development goals
Establish communication protocols with site supervisor and faculty advisor

lecture_notes.introduction: The business internship provides hands-on experience in a professional business environment. This course bridges academic learning and professional practice through structured reflection and mentorship.
key_concepts: Internship as experiential learning: Kolb's learning cycle
Learning agreement: specific goals tied to business competencies
Professional development objectives: technical skills, soft skills, industry knowledge
Site supervisor and faculty advisor roles and expectations
Time tracking and documentation requirements
lecture_notes.demonstration: Developing SMART internship learning goals: each student drafts three professional development objectives with measurable outcomes

review.value: A formal document signed by student, site supervisor, and faculty advisor specifying learning objectives, activities, evaluation criteria, and schedule
Concrete experience, reflective observation, abstract conceptualization, active experimentation; the internship provides the experience, reflection exercises complete the cycle

class_schedule.topic: Professional Skills Workshop: Workplace Communication and Networking
learning_objectives: Apply professional communication skills in workplace contexts
Build a professional network within the internship organization
Navigate workplace culture and unwritten norms

lecture_notes.introduction: The first weeks of an internship set the tone for the entire experience. Making strong first impressions, communicating professionally, and building relationships are critical.
key_concepts: Workplace communication: email tone, meeting etiquette, Slack/Teams norms
Informational interviews: learning from colleagues across the organization
Networking strategy: genuine relationship building, not transactional
Managing up: understanding your supervisor's priorities and communication style
Professional dress code and workplace norms observation
lecture_notes.demonstration: Role-play: navigating common internship scenarios including asking for feedback, clarifying ambiguous assignments, and introducing yourself to senior leaders

review.value: Understand your supervisor's goals, preferred communication style, and priorities; align your work and updates to what matters to them
Request 15-minute conversations with colleagues in different roles to understand the business, show initiative, and build internal network

learning_objectives: Report on internship progress against learning agreement goals
Discuss challenges and strategies for overcoming them
Receive peer feedback and faculty guidance

lecture_notes.introduction: At the midpoint, it is important to assess your progress, recalibrate goals if needed, and address any challenges before the second half of the internship.
key_concepts: Progress assessment against learning agreement objectives
Common internship challenges: unclear expectations, underutilization, interpersonal dynamics
Strategies for seeking stretch assignments
Requesting formal feedback from supervisor
Adjusting learning goals based on experience
lecture_notes.demonstration: Each student presents a 5-minute progress update and receives peer and faculty feedback

review.value: Proactively request projects slightly beyond your current capability; this is where maximum learning occurs and where you demonstrate initiative
Do not wait for your supervisor to give feedback; schedule a mid-point check-in and ask specific questions: 'What am I doing well? Where can I improve?'

class_schedule.topic: Career Development: From Internship to Full-Time
learning_objectives: Leverage internship experience for career advancement
Develop a professional narrative and personal brand
Prepare for post-graduation job search or graduate school
readings: From Intern to Full-Time: Conversion Strategies

lecture_notes.introduction: Many internships lead to full-time offers. Even if they do not, the experience, skills, and network you build are your most valuable career assets.
key_concepts: Internship-to-offer conversion: demonstrating value and expressing interest
Professional narrative: your story of growth, skills, and aspirations
LinkedIn profile optimization and professional online presence
Resume and cover letter updates with internship achievements
Informational interviews for career exploration beyond current company
lecture_notes.demonstration: Elevator pitch workshop: each student crafts and delivers a 60-second professional narrative incorporating internship experience

review.value: Express interest in full-time early and often; ask your supervisor what full-time employees in your role do differently; exceed expectations on every assignment
A concise story connecting your education, internship experience, key accomplishments, and career aspirations into a compelling reason to hire you

learning_objectives: Present internship experience and key learnings to peers and faculty
Submit final internship portfolio with supervisor evaluation
Reflect on professional growth and set post-graduation goals

lecture_notes.introduction: The final session is an opportunity to share your internship experience, celebrate your professional growth, and set goals for the next phase of your career.
key_concepts: Internship presentation: context, role, accomplishments, learnings
Professional growth documentation and evidence
Supervisor evaluation and letter of recommendation
Goal setting for post-graduation professional development
Gratitude and relationship maintenance with internship contacts
lecture_notes.demonstration: Each student delivers a 10-minute final presentation on their internship experience, accomplishments, and key learnings

review.value: Thank your supervisor and colleagues personally; stay in touch quarterly; these relationships are your professional network for years to come
Identify the top 3 skills you need to develop further based on internship experience; create a 6-month development plan

major_assignments.title: Learning Agreement and Professional Development Plan
major_assignments.description: Develop a formal learning agreement with your site supervisor and faculty advisor, including three specific learning objectives, planned activities, and evaluation criteria.
objectives: Define clear, measurable internship learning objectives
Align objectives with business program competencies
Establish evaluation criteria and supervisor communication schedule
deliverables: Signed learning agreement (student, supervisor, faculty advisor)
Three SMART learning objectives with activities

major_assignments.title: Mid-Internship Progress Report and Reflection
major_assignments.description: Submit a mid-point progress report documenting accomplishments, challenges, supervisor feedback, and learning reflections. Include updated goals if objectives have been adjusted.
objectives: Document progress against learning agreement goals
Reflect on professional growth and challenges

criteria: Uses Kolb's cycle: experience, reflection, conceptualization, experimentation
Time requirements met (minimum 60 hours at mid-point)

major_assignments.title: Final Internship Presentation
major_assignments.description: Deliver a 10-minute presentation to peers and faculty summarizing your internship experience, key accomplishments, professional growth, and lessons learned.
objectives: Present internship experience in a compelling professional format
Demonstrate specific accomplishments and value delivered
Articulate professional growth and key learnings
Connect internship experience to career goals

criteria: Clear description of role and organization

major_assignments.title: Final Internship Portfolio
major_assignments.description: Submit a comprehensive internship portfolio including final reflection essay, complete time log, supervisor final evaluation, work samples (if permitted), and updated resume.
objectives: Document the complete internship experience comprehensively
Demonstrate professional growth through evidence and reflection
Update professional materials based on internship experience
deliverables: Work samples or project descriptions (with employer permission)

criteria: Specific examples linked to learning objectives

learning_agreement.description: Learning agreement quality and professional development plan

mid_internship_report.description: Progress report, reflection, and supervisor mid-point evaluation

final_presentation.description: Final internship presentation to peers and faculty

final_portfolio.description: Comprehensive portfolio including reflection essay and documentation

supervisor_evaluation.description: Site supervisor's assessment of intern performance and professionalism

course_policies.attendance: Mandatory for all seminar sessions. Internship site attendance per learning agreement schedule.
course_policies.late_submissions: Portfolio components penalized 10% per day. Supervisor evaluations must be submitted by deadline.
course_policies.collaboration: Internship work is individual. Seminar discussions are collaborative and supportive.
course_policies.academic_integrity: Confidential employer information must be handled per NDA. Work samples require employer permission for inclusion.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Time tracking tool. Reflection journal (digital or physical). Presentation software.
